sections.paragraph:
τέλος, ους, τό [IE]

—1. ‘a point in time that marks culmination’

—a. w. focus on termination, end Mt 24:6, 13f; Mk 3:26; 13:7, 13; Lk 1:33; 21:9; J 13:1; 1 Cor 1:8; 1 Pt 4:7. With οὐκ Lk 1:33. Adv. ἕως τέλους until the end 2 Cor 1:13 (some render ‘fully’).

—b. w. focus on culminating mode, outcome, end Mt 26:58; Lk 22:37; Ro 6:21f (for wordplay, see 2 below); 2 Cor 11:15; Phil 3:19; 1 Ti 1:5; Hb 6:8; 1 Pt 1:9. τὸ τέλος ϰυρίου the Lord’s ending, viz. of Job’s long trial Js 5:11. εἶτα τὸ τέλος next the concluding phase 1 Cor 15:24.

—c. w. focus on aspect of completion, end Ro 10:4. By metonymy, τὸ τέλος as part of a title signifiying the one who brings everything to completion, of God Rv 21:6; of Christ 22:13. Adv. phrases, finally: εἰς τέλος Mt 10:22; Lk 18:5; τὸ δὲ τέλος 1 Pt 3:8.

—2. revenue, tax Mt 17:25; Ro 13:7; in wordplay 6:21f.
